element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player learns top-down perspective movement and interaction with NPCs in towns
Chopping down trees to clear paths; weapon used outside of battle for environmental interaction
Player learns to approach visible enemy sprites and optionally engage; removes randomness
Four-option menu: attack, spell, item, defend; speed determines turn order
Detonating bombs to open sealed doorways; puzzle-solving progression
Clearing wide gaps; platforming element introduced via exploration tool
Stealing/recovery of four elemental crystals; core quest objective
Moving pillars of ice across levels; spatial reasoning with vertical traversal
Manual vs. computer-controlled companion; introduced via battle menu
Gray arrow blocks indicate gated progress; unlocked by dungeon completion
Vitality, attack, defense, speed, magic, accuracy, evasion; automatic equipment replacement
Spells awarded from cleaning battlefields (10 consecutive battles per location)

typical_relief_triggers: Ability to save at any time during exploration
Completion of a dungeon unlocking new world paths
Cleaning out a battlefield for guaranteed reward
Access to new tools that open previous dead-ends
typical_tension_triggers: Visible enemy sprites in dungeons (approach = commitment to battle)
Stationary battlefield encounters (10 battles required per location)
Gray arrow world map restrictions (gating progression)
Boss encounters tied to crystal recovery
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Relaxed entry-level pacing with gentle difficulty curve; anytime save removes save-point tension; visible enemies allow player agency in encounter timing; progression gated by dungeon completion rather than arbitrary level walls

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Grayed-out arrow icons on world map indicate blocked paths; accessible only after prerequisite dungeon completion

difficulty_curve.early_game: Very gentle; focus on learning action-adventure tool interactions (axe, bombs, grappling hook) in safe exploration context
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Gradual escalation; multi-stage dungeon puzzles introduce spatial reasoning; ally control management becomes relevant
notable_spikes: Introduction of each new tool (bomb, grappling hook) increases dungeon complexity
Boss encounters tied to crystal recovery likely represent difficulty peaks

notable_level_patterns.description: Dungeons contain visible enemy sprites that player can choose to approach. Avoidable encounters. Defeat triggers turn-based combat with four-option menu (attack/spell/item/defend).
Tool-based progression: axe to chop trees, bombs to open doors, grappling hook to cross gaps. Puzzles span multiple vertical levels (e.g., Falls Basin ice pillar positioning).
Designated battlefield locations require 10 consecutive enemy victories to 'clean out.' Reward: large EXP, large GP, armor, or spell. Decoupled from main story progression.
Player travels along set icon-to-icon paths. Gray arrows block routes until dungeon completion unlocks them. No free roaming; linear gating.
Core plot progression: recover one of four elemental crystals per dungeon. Likely contains boss encounter and tool-based environmental puzzles.
